rows.notes: Clear centered year at top of page, no amounts present; very legible.
Clear section heading underlined across the page; no monetary figures on same line.
Description legible. Amount aligns in the right-hand columns as 10 shillings 8 pence (pounds column blank). Vertical ruling and nearby rows make column alignment clear.
Description readable though surname slightly indistinct. Single number '11' appears under shillings column (no pence); alignment clear.
Place name and 'Postage and Stamps' readable. Amount reads as 5s 3d in shillings/pence columns; vertical alignment consistent with other 2-number rows.
Indented sub-item under Gwynnear. Amount shows a single '4' aligned with shillings column; pence absent. Some faintness around the text so reading of the description and exact punctuation uncertain.
Abbreviated 'Commrs' (commissioners) readable. Amount appears as 12s 8d; alignment with shillings/pence columns clear.
Second (indented) postage entry under same place; amount reads clearly as 5s 2d in shillings/pence columns.
Indented 'Inn Bill' with three-part amount '4.2.10' clearly written; interpreted as £4 2s 10d. Column alignment and digits clear.
Indented 'Commrs' with three-part amount '11.19.6' clearly visible; interpreted as £11 19s 6d. Good column alignment confirms reading.
Right-hand total for the bracketed Gwynnear group shown as '33 0 9' (pounds, shillings, pence). The brace marks this as a subtotal; digits are well-aligned with column rules.
Text reads 'Rose posts' (legible). Single '3' appears in the shillings column (no pence). Column alignment consistent.
Clear entry 'Draining' with a two-number amount '45 3'. Because '45' > 19 it must be pounds (£45 3s). Good alignment and legible ink.
Surname is somewhat indistinct; read as 'Bratcher. Smith'. Amount printed '1 13' interpreted as £1 13s (two numbers align in pounds/shillings). Name reading uncertain from handwriting.
Description 'Thatching' clear. Amount '4 13' interpreted as £4 13s; alignment under pounds/shillings columns consistent and digits are legible.
Clear word 'Improvement' with amount '2 1' interpreted as £2 1s. Good contrast and column alignment.
Item reads 'Braine pump. Wheeler' and the amount '2 2 3' is clearly written; interpreted as £2 2s 3d with strong column alignment.
Description 'Garden Gate' clear. Amount reads as '2 6' and aligns with shillings/pence columns (10s+ would be in pounds column if intended), so interpreted as 2s 6d.
Name 'Garratt' and 'Bills' legible. Amount '4 12' interpreted as £4 12s given column alignment for two-number values and digit spacing.
Reads 'Badger Impro & Collecting (Cott Rents)' (parenthetical abbreviation). Amount '40 11' must be £40 11s (40 > 19). Clear alignment and digits.
Place/name 'Wolgrove' readable. Amount '5 1 3' (three numbers) clearly visible and fits pounds/shillings/pence columns.
Handwritten name/annotation is noisy; read as 'Kimber j cost gig House'. Amount '4 9 3' fits the three-column format (£4 9s 3d). Name uncertain due to cramped script.
Small item 'poles' with amount printed as '10 6'. Context and column alignment indicate this is 10s 6d (pounds column blank).
Clearly reads 'Painting - weather'. Amount '1 9 6' is unambiguous as £1 9s 6d; digits align well with columns.

Name reads as 'Turrill - Honor' but handwriting is moderately unclear. Amount appears as '3 17' which must be £3 17s (two-number format). Slight uncertainty on exact surname and whether any pence were intended but not written.
Name 'Hartwell' readable. Amount '3 4 10' interpreted as £3 4s 10d; digits and columns clear though ink slightly lighter than surrounding lines.
Surname 'Jones' legible. Amount appears to be '1 7 8' (£1 7s 8d) though middle digit is a little faded — column position supports this interpretation.
Word reads like 'Chancel' (handwriting ambiguous). Amount appears as '1 7 9' (£1 7s 9d) but some ink overlap and shadowing near the line make the pence digit slightly uncertain.
Clear name 'Keeley' and 'repairs'. Amount '9 12 6' is legible and sits squarely in the three monetary columns (£9 12s 6d).
Acts as a subheading for the following lines; 'Kidlington Cottages Comm.' is readable though abbreviated. No amount printed on this line.

Indented 'Rands Bill' is legible. Amount shows '1 9' — interpreted as £1 9s (two-number format). Could alternatively be 1s 9d but surrounding items of similar style use two numbers for £/s when the left digit >0, so read as £1 9s.
Reads 'Robson Do' (i.e., Robson ditto). Amount '1 3 4' clearly visible as £1 3s 4d; digits and columns are clear.
Name appears as 'Lymath' (handwriting slightly stylized). Amount '4 10' interpreted as £4 10s given column alignment; pence not present.
Entry 'Osborne Bill' readable. Amount '10 14 11' clearly written in pounds/shillings/pence columns as £10 14s 11d.
Clearly reads 'J. Newton Cottage repairs'. Amount '4 12' interpreted as £4 12s; digits and alignment are clear.
Name 'Faulkner. Davis' legible. Amount '35 4 5' clearly visible and must be £35 4s 5d (35>19 indicates pounds).
Surname 'Hartley' clear. Amount reads '15 14 6' which is unambiguous as £15 14s 6d; alignment clear.
Name is somewhat unclear (read as 'Drust'); amount '7 2 10' appears as £7 2s 10d. The letters of the name are faded and slightly ambiguous, hence moderate confidence.
Final page total clearly written in the bottom-right corner as '263 8 9' (£263 8s 9d). Alignment with ruling lines and clarity of digits is excellent.
